2606.30561.title: The Human Creativity Benchmark
2606.30561.paper_summary: Modern AI evaluation frameworks treat evaluator disagreement as noise to be resolved. In creative domains, professional disagreement reflects genuine differences in taste, not measurement error. We argue that evaluating creative AI requires preserving two distinct signals: convergence, where professionals align around shared best practices, and divergence, where individual taste legitimately varies. We present the Human Creativity Benchmark (HCB), a benchmark that operationalizes this separation by collecting pairwise preferences, scalar ratings on prompt adherence, usability, and visual appeal, and qualitative rationale from domain professionals. Across 15,000 professional judgments spanning five creative domains and three workflow phases (ideation, mockup, refinement), we find that convergence concentrates on verifiable dimensions like technical correctness and visual hierarchy, while divergence concentrates on taste-driven dimensions like aesthetic direction and conceptual risk. No model excels uniformly across all phases. Collapsing these signals into a single quality metric discards the most actionable information: where models must be correct versus where they should remain steerable.
2606.30561.paper_summary_zh: 現代的人工智慧評估框架將評估者之間的分歧視為需要解決的噪音。在創意領域，專業的分歧反映了真正的品味差異，而非測量誤差。我們主張，評估創意人工智慧需要保留兩個不同的信號：趨同，即專業人士圍繞共享最佳實踐達成一致，以及分歧，即個人品味的合法變化。我們提出了人類創意基準（HCB），這是一個通過收集成對偏好、對提示遵循性、可用性和視覺吸引力的標量評分，以及來自領域專業人士的定性理由來操作化這一區分的基準。在涵蓋五個創意領域和三個工作流程階段（構思、模型、精煉）的15,000個專業判斷中，我們發現趨同集中於可驗證的維度，如技術正確性和視覺層次，而分歧則集中於以品味驅動的維度，如美學方向和概念風險。沒有任何模型在所有階段中均表現出色。將這些信號合併為單一質量指標會丟失最具可行性的信息：模型必須正確的地方與應保持可調整的地方。
2606.30561.author: Aspen Hopkins et.al.
2606.30561.authors: Aspen Hopkins, Allison Nulty, Alexandria Minetti, Anoop Pakki, Angad Singh
